status.note: Manual editorial status based on the sources below; this is not medical monitoring.

sources.title: Wangchuk begins hunger strike at Jantar Mantar, khap leaders join CJP protest
supports: The fast began at Jantar Mantar on 28 June 2026.

sources.title: Sonam Wangchuk: Activist on hunger strike at CJP protest loses 8.2kg
supports: BBC described him as alive after sixteen days and reported 8.2 kg weight loss.

sources.title: Sonam Wangchuk Hunger Strike 2026: Why He Is Protesting
supports: On 14 July he was 17 days into a reported water-only fast, had lost more than 8 kg, and had medics in attendance.
As of 15 July, Frontline said no Union government official had met the protesters.

sources.title: Government cruel for ignoring Wangchuk's fast, says Dipke, as activist completes day 18 of hunger strike
supports: He completed day 18 on 15 July and was reported under 24-hour medical vigilance.

sources.title: Peaceful march to the Parliament, 20th July
supports: Wangchuk referred to his ongoing hunger strike and announced a 20 July Parliament march.

sources.title: Efficacy and safety of prolonged water fasting: a narrative review of human trials
supports: Human prolonged-fasting trials report marked rises in circulating ketones.
Blood glucose and blood pressure can change during fasting but are nonspecific metabolic and haemodynamic measures, not proof of continuous food abstention.

sources.title: Long-Term Fasting-Induced Ketosis in 1610 Subjects: Metabolic Regulation and Safety
supports: The study monitored fasting ketosis with urine acetoacetate and, in a subset, blood beta-hydroxybutyrate.
Ketone responses varied between individuals and were affected by small carbohydrate-containing supplements and activity.

sources.publisher: Diabetes Technology & Therapeutics via PubMed Central
sources.title: Update on Measuring Ketones
supports: Blood beta-hydroxybutyrate and urine acetoacetate document ketone production through different methods.
Ketosis can occur with fasting, very-low-carbohydrate intake, exercise, exogenous ketones, insulin deficiency and other contexts; a ketone result alone does not prove a continuous water-only fast.
